title: AI in Cultural Collections
text:
This is a rough draft website, intended to provide a toolkit for researchers working with Cultural Collections in the use of AI.
The aim of this web resource is to provide an evolving source of guidance for ethical use of AI in research.
The guide is split into two main sections:

Guidance: principles to consider and follow when considering implementing AI into your workflow. The guidance is split between guidelines for individual readers, and for institutions.
Toolkit: practical tools and resources to help you implement the guidance above.

The current version of the guidance that you’re viewing is: v0.1.0.
This website is designed to be easily forked (copied) and modified for your institution.

text:
Practical guidance, workflows, and project notes for using AI with cultural collections.

title: Key Principle 1: Understand AI
text:
While the jargon associated with AI can be challenging, it’s of key importance to understand what exactly different models and approaches are doing under the covers so that you can be fully aware of the applicability of different models and the in-built assumptions involved.
Using a commercial generative AI chatbot to generate a workflow is extremely far removed from using a custom-built machine learning model to identify features in images, yet both are now classed as applications of AI.
